description:
You are a deployment engineer collecting quick health-check metrics right after pushing new application code.  
Inside the workspace you will find the file:

  /home/user/deployment/server.log

That log already exists and contains many timestamped status messages, some of which include the literal, all-uppercase keyword “UPDATED”.  

Your objective is to create a single new text file that records how many log entries mention that keyword.

1. Create the directory  
  /home/user/deployment/metrics  
   if it does not already exist.

2. Generate a file named  
  /home/user/deployment/metrics/updated_count.log

3. The file must contain exactly one line, in the following **precise** format (no leading/trailing spaces):

   UPDATED_COUNT=N

   where N is the total number of lines inside /home/user/deployment/server.log that contain the exact, case-sensitive substring “UPDATED”.  
   A POSIX newline character (0x0A) must follow the number; the file must end with that newline.

Example of the required layout (for illustration only—your value of N will depend on the actual log contents):

   UPDATED_COUNT=17⏎

4. Achieve steps 1–3 using a **single compound shell command** (you may combine utilities with pipes, redirections, &&, etc.).  
   Do not manually open an editor; the count should be computed automatically from the file contents.

The automated test will verify:

• The directory /home/user/deployment/metrics exists.  
• The file /home/user/deployment/metrics/updated_count.log exists.  
• The file contains exactly one line whose format is UPDATED_COUNT=integer and ends with a newline.  
• The integer equals the true count of lines containing the case-sensitive word “UPDATED” in /home/user/deployment/server.log.

Report “Done” in the terminal when finished.
truth:
Initial state provided to the agent:

1. Path /home/user/deployment/ exists and is writable by the user.
2. File /home/user/deployment/server.log exists with the following 12 lines (each terminated by a newline character):

[2024-05-01 10:01:03] STARTUP complete
[2024-05-01 10:02:17] UPDATED service-A to v1.4.2
[2024-05-01 10:03:08] Health-check passed
[2024-05-01 10:05:44] UPDATED service-B to v3.2.1
[2024-05-01 10:07:09] NOTICE scaling event
[2024-05-01 10:10:22] UPDATED cache-layer configuration
[2024-05-01 10:12:56] INFO background job started
[2024-05-01 10:15:31] UPDATED database schema migration
[2024-05-01 10:18:47] WARN high memory usage
[2024-05-01 10:20:00] COMPLETED rollout script
[2024-05-01 10:21:15] UPDATED monitoring alerts
[2024-05-01 10:22:59] SHUTDOWN initiated

Total count of lines containing “UPDATED”: 5

Expected final artefacts after successful task completion:

• Directory /home/user/deployment/metrics/ exists.
• File /home/user/deployment/metrics/updated_count.log exists and its exact byte content is:
UPDATED_COUNT=5\n

(where “\n” represents a single Unix newline character). No other characters, spaces, or lines should be present.
